name: spotifython

metadata.name: spotifython
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: A caching python interface to readonly parts of the spotify api.

documentation:
# spotifython

A readonly wrapper for the Spotify API that relies on heavy caching to minimise the number of requests.

## Key Features

- caching requested data (note that non public data will be cached)

## Installation

**python 3.10 or higher is required**

```sh
# Linux/macOS
python3 -m pip install -U spotifython
```

```sh
# Windows
py -3 -m pip install -U spotifython
```

To install the development version, run:

```sh
$ git clone https://github.com/vawvaw/spotifython
$ cd spotipython
$ python3 -m pip install -U .
```

## Quick Example

```py
import spotifython

if __name__ == "__main__":
    scope = spotifython.Scope(playlist_read_private=True, user_library_read=True)
    authentication = spotifython.Authentication(
        client_id="client_id",
        client_secret="client_secret",
        scope=scope
    )
    client = spotifython.Client(authentication=authentication)

    playlists = client.user_playlists()
    for playlist in playlists:
        print(playlist.name)

    client.close()
```

## Links

- [Documentation](https://spotifython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)
- [Spotify API Documentation](https://developer.spotify.com/documentation/web-api/)

classes.name: spotifython.authentication.Authentication

methods.name: spotifython.authentication.Authentication.to_dict
documentation:
Call this function before exiting and store the data somewhere safe.

```
return

dict with organised data for caching
```

methods.name: spotifython.authentication.Authentication.from_dict
documentation:
---
param data: dict generated in `to_dict()`
return: Authentication
---

Store the data needed to interact with the Spotify API. Not all arguments are needed. Options are (client_id, client_secret, scope), (client_id, client_secret, scope, refresh_token), (token, token_expires)

```
param client_id

the Client ID of the application

param client_secret

the Client Secret of the application (click on "SHOW CLIENT SECRET")

param scope

the Scope object or string reflecting the permissions you need

param show_dialog

whether to query the user every time a new refresh token is requested

param token_expires

timestamp when the token expires
```

classes.name: spotifython.client.Client

methods.name: spotifython.client.Client.get_authentication_data
documentation:
Dump the authentication data for safe caching

```
return

{ "client_id": client_id, "client_secret": client_secret, "scope": str(scope), "refresh_token": refresh_token, "show_dialog": show_dialog, "token": token, "expires": int(expires) }
```

methods.name: spotifython.client.Client.play
documentation:
resume playback or play specified resource
only one of albums and context may be specified

```
param elements

list of spotify uris or Playable types to play (None to resume playing)

param context

uri or PlayContext to use as context (e.g. playlist or album)

param offset

number of song in resource to start playing (only used if context_uri is set)

param position_ms

position in song to seek (only used if context_uri is set)

param device_id

device to target (None to use currently active device

raises SpotifyException

errors according to http response status
```

params.name: Playlist | User | Episode | Track | Album | Artist | Show | SavedTracks

documentation:
search for item

```
param query

string to search

param element_type

comma-separated list of return types; possible values: "album" "artist" "playlist" "track" "episode" "show"

param limit

number of results to return per type

param offset

offset of results per type

return

dict with types as keys and lists as albums
```

standard interface to the api

```
param authentication

Authentication object for client authentication

param cache_dir

global path to the directory that this library should cache data in (note that sensitive data you request may be cached, set to None to disable caching)
```
